title: Your First TS Program
description: Write and understand a basic typed program.

slides.title: Hello Type World 🌍
slides.content:
Let's dive in and write a basic TypeScript program.

The syntax looks exactly like JavaScript initially.

slides.title: A Simple Greeting
slides.content: Here is a standard variable and function in TypeScript:

quizData.question: Is the code above valid TypeScript?

options.text: Yes, valid JavaScript is valid TypeScript.
Yes, but only if compiled with special flags.

quizData.explanation: Because TypeScript is a superset of JavaScript, regular JS code is completely valid TS code without changing anything.

slides.title: The Sneaky Helper 🕵️
slides.content:
Even though our `greeting` variable doesn't explicitly look like it has a type, TypeScript is silently doing some magic in the background.

It looks at `"Hello World!"` and infers that `greeting` must be a `string`.

If we try to assign `greeting = 42;` immediately after, TypeScript will complain with an error!

slides.title: The Error
slides.content: TypeScript will stop us from doing this:
slides.codeSnippet:
let greeting = "Hello World!";

greeting = 42; // TypeScript Error: Type 'number' is not assignable to type 'string'.
